text: The antitumor role of KLF4 in colorectal cancer is evidently clear. KLF4 was downregulated in human colorectal cancer tissues as compared with normal mucosa [ 80 ]. KLF4 acted as a tumor suppressor to inhibit the colorectal cancer growth in mice [ 81 ]. KLF4 inhibited colorectal cancer cell proliferation through upregulating p21 and NDRG2 (a molecule downstream of Myc), and downregulating cyclinD1 (the key molecule of cell cycle G1/S extension), GINS complex subunit 4 (GINS4), and IFITM3 (a tumor metastasis-associated molecule) [ 80 , 82 , 83 ]. Conversely, KLF4 deficiency accelerated the progression of colitis and colon cancer [ 84 ]. There were multiple microRNAs downregulating KLF4, including microRNA-92a and microRNA-10b, promoting colorectal cancer growth, migration, proliferation, and metastasis [ 85 , 86 ]. KLF4 deficiency is also associated with tumor development by EMT [ 87 ]. These evidences elucidate an antitumor role of KLF4 as a tumor suppressor in colorectal cancer.
summary: KLF4 acts as a tumor suppressor in colorectal cancer and is downregulated in human colorectal cancer tissues compared with normal mucosa. KLF4 inhibited colorectal cancer growth in mice. KLF4 upregulated p21 and NDRG2. It downregulated cyclinD1, GINS4, and IFITM3 to inhibit colorectal cancer cell proliferation. KLF4 deficiency accelerated progression of colitis and colon cancer and was associated with EMT-driven tumor development. MicroRNAs including microRNA-92a and microRNA-10b downregulated KLF4 and promoted colorectal cancer growth, migration, proliferation, and metastasis.

text: Currently, a few compounds have been designed to target the tumor-regulating effects of KLF4, and are available for clinical trials. APTO-253, a targeted inducer of KLF4, have been found in a clinical trial to have successfully halted the progression of some advanced tumors, especially leukemia [ 173 ]. However, the company indicated that this drug inhibited MYC and had discontinued the clinical development. In a clinical trial, metformin had a positive effect on colorectal cancer and endometrial cancer by increasing KLF4 expression [ 146 ]. Also, KLF4 can be negatively regulated by the cis-structural transcription factor CDX2. In colon cells, CDX2 upregulated KLF4 by binding to KLF4 promoter and reducing the association of histone 3 lysine 4 trimethylation with KDM5B demethylase, and acting as a tumor suppressor. However, transcription factors are generally considered undruggable. Therefore, it is premature to consider KLF4 as a general target for cancer therapy.
summary: KLF4 is implicated in regulating tumors in colorectal cancer. In a clinical trial metformin increased KLF4 expression and had a positive effect on colorectal cancer. APTO-253, a targeted inducer of KLF4, halted progression of some advanced tumors in a clinical trial but was later discontinued after the company indicated it inhibited MYC. In colon cells CDX2 upregulated KLF4 by binding the KLF4 promoter and by reducing histone 3 lysine 4 trimethylation association with KDM5B demethylase. CDX2 therefore acted as a tumor suppressor. Because transcription factors are generally considered undruggable it is premature to consider KLF4 a general target for cancer therapy.

text: Although the incidence of PDA is not as high as that of breast, lung and colorectal cancers, the mortality rate is almost as high as the morbidity rate due to the lack of effective treatment. The current measures for PDA management have shifted to early diagnosis and treatment. In PDA, KLF4 promoted the formation of precancerous lesions in mouse models, including ADM and PanIN. Human PDA was associated with chronic pancreatitis, in which ADM was observed [ 88 ], and ADM regulated the formation and progression of PanIN and PDA [ 89 , 90 ]. Notably, KLF4 was increased in ADM and PanIN in mouse models and played a key role in the formation of ADM and PanIN. Knockdown of KLF4 significantly slowed down the formation of ADM and PanIN, while overexpression of KLF4 promoted the formation and progression of AMD and PanIN [ 91 , 92 ].
